title: Try It Yourself - Unstage the file

content.problemStatement:
**In your workspace** : 
- `myFile.txt` is already created and committed (initial commit done).
- One wrong change is done in the file and accidentally added to the staging area. 

Use `git status` to check, You'll see : \
**"Changes to be committed"**


- You don’t want to commit this change yet -> So you can unstage this changes.

---

**To Unstage the file :** \
Open terminal and run :
```sss
git restore --staged myFile.txt
```
- The changes you made stay in the file
- The file is removed from the staging area

If you check `git status`, You'll see : \
**"Changes not staged for commit"**


---
